---
title: "Sam Wiwatanapataphee (she/her)"
subtitle: | 
        PhD (Maths & Stats), lecturer, and data scientist
image: /assets/images/image.png
format:
  html:
    grid:
      body-width: 800px
about:
  id: my-about
  template: solana
  image: /assets/images/me-0-crop.jpg
  image-width: 9em
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - text: '{{< iconify fa7-solid:envelope >}} Email'
      href: mailto:dwiwat97@gmail.com
      aria-label: Envelope logo for email
    - text: '{{< iconify lucide:university >}} Curtin Profile'
      href: https://staffportal.curtin.edu.au/staff/profile/view/sam-wiwatanapataphee-0f5d3774/
      aria-label: Curtin Staff Profile
      target: _blank
    - text: '{{< iconify fa7-brands:orcid title="ORCID logo" label="ORCID logo" >}} ORCID'
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0003-2966-5084
      aria-label: ORCID logo
      target: _blank
      rel: noopener noreferrer
    - text: '{{< iconify fa7-brands:linkedin >}} LinkedIn'
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/dwiwat97/
      aria-label: LinkedIn logo
      target: _blank
      rel: noopener noreferrer
    - text: '{{< iconify octicon:mark-github-16 >}} GitHub'
      href: https://github.com/zamnolence
      aria-label: GitHub logo
      target: _blank
      rel: noopener noreferrer

---

:::: {#my-about}
:::


:::: {.about-body}


I am a lecturer in AI & data science specialising in data-driven optimisation and computer simulation using Python, R, and MATLAB. I completed a PhD in Mathematics and Statistics with research spanning fractional differential equations, space–time modelling, and blockchain-based smart grid systems.

Currently, I focus on fostering an inclusive, project-based learning environment that connects mathematical and statistical theory with real-world applications that highlight sustainability and responsible decision-making.


## {{< iconify octicon:briefcase-24 >}} Work Experience

::: {.callout-tip icon="false"}
### <span style="font-size: 1.5em; margin-left: .5em;">{{< iconify lucide:university >}} &nbsp; Curtin University, Australia</span>

::: {style="font-size: 1.1em;"}
| | |
|:--------------|----:|
| **Associate Lecturer**<br><span style="color: #979797;">I'm currently teaching the unit *Computer Simulation* (onshore), *Economics* (offshore), and *Supply Chain Modelling and Optimisation* (offshore) and supervising 12 UG/MS students.</span> | 2026 - Present |
| **Sessional Academic**<br><span style="color: #979797;">Teaching and tutoring 600+ UG students onshore and offshore across nine units in maths, stats, data science, and finance, including:<br></span> | 2023 - 2025 |
| **Research Assistant**<br><span style="color: #979797;">Solving DEs with FEM for bridge structural analysis.<br> Output was published in 2019.</span> | Feb - Nov 2018 |

: {.borderless .hover}
:::

:::

::: {.callout-tip icon="false"}
### <span style="font-size: 1.5em; margin-left: .5em;">{{< iconify streamline:office-worker >}} &nbsp; Thanachart Insurance PCL, Thailand</span>

::: {style="font-size: 1.1em;"}
| | |
|:----------------|----:|
| **Intern Analyst**<br><span style="color: #979797;">Conducted advanced statistical research on motor insurance risk, customer retention, and renewal rates;<br>Developed Tableau dashboards for KPI monitoring and insights.</span> | 2018 - 2019 |

: {.borderless .hover}
:::
:::

## {{< iconify octicon:mortar-board-24 >}} Education {.education}

| | |
|:----------------------------------|----:|
| **_Ph.D._ in Mathematics and Statistics**<br>*Curtin Univerity, Australia*<br><span style="color: #979797;">*RTP Scholarship Recipient*<br>**Thesis:** *Blockchain-based Renewable Energy Trading and Storage Optimisation*</span> | 2025 |
| **_Master_ of Data Science** (*Distinction*)<br>*The University of Western Australia, Australia* | 2021 |
| **_Bachelor_ of Science** (*Actuarial Science*)<br>*Curtin University, Australia*<br><span style="color: #979797;">Actuaries Institute Australia's Foundation program<br>• CT1 Financial Mathematics;<br>• CB1 Business Finance;<br>• CS1 Actuarial Statistics<br>• CB2 Business Economics;<br>• CM2 Financial Engineerng & Loss Reserving</span> | 2018 |

: {.borderless .hover}



## {{< iconify octicon:tools-24 >}} Technical Skills

I work in quantitative analysis, statistical modelling, and actuarial methods, supported by experience in data analytics, technical development, and teaching.

<span class="badge rounded-pill bg-light">{{< iconify devicon:python title="Python" label="Python" >}}</span>
<span class="badge rounded-pill bg-light">{{< iconify fa7-brands:r-project title="R" label="R" >}}</span>
<span class="badge rounded-pill bg-light">{{< iconify devicon:matlab title="MATLAB" label="MATLAB" >}}</span>
<span class="badge rounded-pill bg-light">{{< iconify devicon:visualbasic title="VB" label="VB" >}}</span>
<span class="badge rounded-pill bg-light">{{< iconify devicon:git title="Git" label="Git" >}}</span>
<span class="badge rounded-pill bg-light">{{< iconify file-icons:latex title="LaTeX" label="LaTeX" >}}</span>
<span class="badge rounded-pill bg-light">{{< iconify material-icon-theme:quarto title="Quarto" label="Quarto" >}}</span>
<span class="badge rounded-pill bg-light">{{< iconify logos:mysql title="MySQL" label="MySQL" >}}</span>
<span class="badge rounded-pill bg-light">{{< iconify devicon:html5-wordmark title="HTML5" label="HTML5" >}}</span>
<span class="badge rounded-pill bg-light">{{< iconify devicon:css title="CSS" label="CSS" >}}</span>
<span class="badge rounded-pill bg-light">{{< iconify devicon:javascript title="JS" label="JS" >}}</span>
<span class="badge rounded-pill bg-light">{{< iconify devicon:linux title="linux" label="linux" >}}</span>




::: {.callout-tip icon="false"}
### <span style="font-size: 1.5em; margin-left: 1em;">{{< iconify carbon:ibm-software-watsonx-data-analyze-and-process >}} Analytical & Modelling</span>

- Mathematical and statistical modelling
- Machine learning and deep learning models for predictive and data-driven analysis
- Simulation and optimisation, including Monte Carlo simulation and supply chain modelling
- Actuarial modelling, including time series forecasting, stochastic processes, and risk modelling
- Insurance analytics, including loss modelling, customer retention analysis, and KPI/dashboard development

:::


::: {.callout-tip icon="false"}
### <span style="font-size: 1.5em; margin-left: 1em;">{{< iconify hugeicons:teacher >}} Teaching & Academic Practice </span>

- Curriculum design in economics and optimisation; coordination of academic units
- Delivered tutorials, workshops, and mentored students on applied projects
- Experienced with LMS platforms (Blackboard, Moodle) and modern quantitative pedagogy
:::

::: {.callout-tip icon="false"}
### <span style="font-size: 1.5em; margin-left: 1em;">{{< iconify material-symbols:code >}} Techinal & Development </span>

- Web development: HTML, CSS, JavaScript
- Tools & environments: Git, Unix/Linux
- Scientific writing and documentation using LaTeX
:::

## {{< iconify octicon:beaker-24 >}} Research & Publications

My research focuses on integrating mathematical, statistical, and computational methods to address real‑world sustainability challenges, in particular:

- Multifractal Models for Financial Time Series
- Optimisation of Battery Storage Systems using Big Data from Smart Grids 
- ML for Predictive Maintenance in Sustainable Wind and Solar Systems
- AI-Based Financial Risk Management under Market Volatility

| Year | Publication |
|:---:|-------------|
| 2026 | Sawangtong, W., Wiwatanataphee, D. & Sawangtong, P. Extended Caputo Space–Fractional Black–Scholes Equation with Scale-Dependent Diffusion. AIMS Mathmatics, 2026, 11(3): 7468-7496. [https://doi.org/10.3934/math.2026306](https://www.aimspress.com/article/doi/10.3934/math.2026306) | 
| 2025 | Wiwatanapataphee, D., Wu, Y., Sawangtong, W. & Sawangtong, P. Modeling Anomalous Diffusion and Volatility in the Australian National Electricity Market Using a Space-Fractional Black-Scholes Framework[J]. AIMS Mathematics, 2025, 10(5): 12388-12420. [https://doi.org/10.3934/math.2025560](https://doi.org/10.3934/math.2025560) |
| 2019 | Wiwatanapataphee, D., Khajohnsaksumeth, N. & Wu, Y.H. Effect of Beam Joinery on Bridge Structural Stability. Advanced Differential Equation 2019, 225 (2019). [https://doi.org/10.1186/s13662-019-2158-5](https://doi.org/10.1186/s13662-019-2158-5) |
| **Date** | **Conference** |
| 2-6 Feb 2025 | Gave a talk on the topic of "Fractional Black-Scholes Model for Pricing Derivatives in the Australian Electricity Market" at ANZIAM 2025 Conference, Pacific Bay Resort, Coffs Harbour, NSW. |
| 16-18 Dec 2018 | Gave a talk on the topic of "Effect of beam joinery on bridge structural stability" at ICMA-MU 2018: The 2018 International Conference in Mathematics and Applications, The Century Park Hotel, Bangkok, Thailand. |

: {.hover}

## {{< iconify tabler:certificate >}} Certificates

| | | |
|--:|:------------|----------:|
| 2026 | Provide First Aid<br>First Aid for Mental Health | St John Ambulance Western Australia |
| 2026 | Emergency Fire Warden | ALCAN Fire Safety |
| 2024 | AI Intensive Bootcamp | VP Akkodis Academy Australia |
| 2018 | Statistical Tools for Risk Modelling | Thammasat University |

: {.borderless .hover}


:::
